input:
# Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** In-Season Strength + Power Maintenance (2-Day) — Basketball Performance & Ankle Durability  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Foundation + Tissue Tolerance (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Brandon Taylor

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Full Body A — Power + Hinge Strength (performance center, ~2:00–2:45 PM)
- **Tue:** Rest / basketball only
- **Wed:** Rest / basketball only
- **Thu (2/26):** Full Body B — Accel/Power + Squat Pattern Strength (performance center, ~2:00–2:45 PM)
- **Fri:** Rest / basketball only
- **Sat:** Rest / game (as scheduled)
- **Sun:** Off / recovery

## Week Overview
Week 1 is about establishing **repeatable in-season training loads** (conservative RPE ~6–7) and getting crisp power contacts without soreness. We’ll keep jumps/sled **low volume and high quality**, then hit a few strength patterns with short, efficient accessories. Every session includes **ankle/foot capacity + single-leg control** to reduce “giving way.”

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Power + hinge strength; ankle stiffness + controlled unilateral  
**Location:** University performance center  
**Duration:** ~45 minutes

**Warm-Up (8 minutes)**
1. Bike or light jog: **2:00** easy
2. Dynamic lower series (1 round):
   - Leg swings (front/back): **10/side**
   - Leg swings (lateral): **10/side**
   - Walking lunge + reach: **6/side**
3. Ankle activation (1 round):
   - Tibialis raises (wall): **12 reps**
   - Bent-knee calf raises (controlled): **10/side**
4. Landing prep:
   - Snap-down to athletic stance (stick): **2 × 3**

**Main Workout**

### 1. Pogo Jumps (ankle stiffness)
**Target:** 2 × 20 sec @ easy–moderate, quiet contacts  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec  
- **Set 2:** 20 sec  
**Rest:** 40–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture, minimal knee bend, “bounce off the floor,” stop if ankles feel unstable.

### 2. Box Jump (stick landing)
**Target:** 3 × 2 @ RPE 6–7 (quality only)  
- **Set 1:** 24" box × 2 (clean stick)  
- **Set 2:** 24" box × 2 (clean stick)  
- **Set 3:** 24" box × 2 (clean stick)  
**Rest:** ~90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Step down (don’t jump down). Land midfoot, knees track over toes, control wobble.

### 3. Trap Bar Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 5 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 185 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 225 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 255 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 255 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 6:** 255 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2:30–3:00 between working sets  
**Notes:** Smooth, no grinders. Brace first, push the floor away. If practice legs feel heavy this week, drop to **245** for all working sets.

### 4. DB Bench Press (neutral grip if preferred)
**Target:** 3 × 6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50s × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 60s × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 70s × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 4:** 70s × 6 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 5:** 70s × 6 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Fast concentric. Stop 1–2 reps shy of failure to avoid soreness.

### 5. Chest-Supported Row (DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 55s × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 55s × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 55s × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 1-sec squeeze at the top; no shrugging.

### 6. Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (controlled)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** DBs 30s × 6/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** DBs 30s × 6/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets (alternate legs)  
**Notes:** 2-sec down, light touch, drive up. Keep this *easy* Week 1—this is the biggest DOMS risk.

### 7. Ankle Stability Superset (quick)
**Target:** 2 rounds  
- **A) Tibialis Raises:** 15 reps (bodyweight)  
- **B) Single-Leg Balance:** 25 sec/side (eyes forward)  
**Rest:** ~30 sec between moves  
**Notes:** On balance: tripod foot (big toe, little toe, heel). If ankle feels “loose,” shorten time and add support with a fingertip.

**Cool Down (4 minutes)**
1. Hip flexor stretch: **30 sec/side**  
2. Hamstring stretch (banded or toe touch hold): **30 sec/side**  
3. Calf stretch (knee straight): **30 sec/side**  
4. Soleus stretch (knee bent): **30 sec/side**

**Notes**
- Keep this session snappy—leave feeling better than you came in.  
- If legs are beat from weekend games, reduce trap bar to **2 working sets** and keep jumps the same.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Accel intent + squat pattern strength; pull strength; foot/ankle robustness  
**Location:** University performance center  
**Duration:** ~45 minutes

**Warm-Up (8 minutes)**
1. Row or bike: **2:00** easy
2. Movement prep (1 round):
   - High knees: **20 yd**
   - Butt kicks: **20 yd**
   - Lateral shuffle: **10 yd each way**
3. Ankle/foot prep (1 round):
   - Single-leg pogos in place: **10/side** (low amplitude)
   - Heel-elevated bodyweight squat: **8 reps** (easy ROM)
4. Front rack prep:
   - Empty bar front squat hold in rack position: **2 × 10 sec**

**Main Workout**

### 1. Sled Push (acceleration emphasis)
**Target:** 5 × 12 m @ heavy but fast (no grind), full walk-back rest  
- **Set 1:** 180 lb total on sled × 12 m (fast)  
- **Set 2:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 3:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 4:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 12 m  
**Rest:** Walk-back + 45–60 sec (full recovery)  
**Notes:** Forward shin angle, punch the ground back, torso ~45°. If speed drops, reduce to **160**.

### 2. Front Squat
**Target:** 3 × 5 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 95 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 135 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 6:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2:30 between working sets  
**Notes:** Upright torso, elbows high. This is a “practice strength” set—no grinding.

### 3. Chin-Ups (or Pull-Ups)
**Target:** 3 × 6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full hang to chin over bar; if you miss reps, switch to **band-assisted** and finish the rep target.

### 4. DB Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70s × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 2:** 70s × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hinge back, lats tight, stop at mid-shin (no stretch-chasing).

### 5. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb (bar + 10) × 6/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 45 lb (bar + 10) × 6/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ribcage down, squeeze glute on kneeling side, press slightly up-and-forward.

### 6. Foot/Ankle Finisher
**Target:** 2 rounds  
- **A) Soleus Isometric (bent-knee calf raise hold):** 25 sec/side  
- **B) Single-Leg Calf Raise (controlled):** 8 reps/side @ bodyweight  
**Rest:** 30 sec between moves  
**Notes:** Soleus burn is normal; keep ankle stacked (don’t roll outward).

**Cool Down (4 minutes)**
1. Quad stretch: **30 sec/side**  
2. Adductor rock-backs: **8 reps/side**  
3. Lat/pec stretch (doorway or rig): **30 sec/side**  
4. Ankle dorsiflexion wall rocks: **10/side** (gentle)

**Notes**
- If you have a game within 24–36 hours, keep front squat at **2 working sets** and skip DB RDL.  
- Goal is leaving with legs feeling “charged,” not cooked.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Mon (Power + Hinge):** Established conservative baselines (Trap Bar **255×5×3**, DB Bench **70s×6×3**) with low-soreness single-leg work and ankle stability.
- **Thu (Accel + Squat):** Sled work stayed speed-focused (5×12 m @ **180 lb**), front squat baseline **165×5×3**, pulling volume kept moderate for recovery.
- **Constraint monitoring (ankle + in-season fatigue):** Daily ankle work included (tibialis + balance + soleus). Priority this week: **no wobble landings, no grinders**, and keep split squat/RDL controlled to limit DOMS.
- **Weak points (durability):** Consistent ankle stiffness (pogos) + soleus capacity (isos) + unilateral control (RFESS) baked in without adding volume.
- **Next week (Week 2):** If practices feel normal and soreness is minimal, add **+1 rep per set** on trap bar (to **3×6**) *or* add **+5 lb** while staying at 3×5; keep jumps/sled volume the same and progress by crispness.
context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Brandon Taylor  
- **Age:** 22  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — ~3 years consistent lifting; D3 college basketball athlete, currently in-season (as of **2026-02-27**)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **In-season performance support** (as of **2026-02-27**)  
  - Maintain/build **strength & power** without excessive soreness  
  - Improve/maintain **explosiveness** (jump/first-step)  
  - Enhance **durability** and **injury prevention** during remaining season
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Manage **ankle stability** and reduce risk of lower-limb injuries
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **In-season window:** ~**12 weeks remaining** (from **2026-02-27**)

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon + Thu**
- **Desired training frequency:** **2 sessions/week**
- **Session duration:** **45 minutes**
- **Time windows:** **2:00 PM** (between classes and practice)
- **Key scheduling constraint:** Programming must minimize DOMS/fatigue that interferes with practices/games

### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Commercial gym / **university athletic performance center**
- **Available equipment (confirmed):**
  - Barbells, dumbbells
  - Racks, platforms
  - Sled
  - Plyo boxes

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Chronic ankle instability — tapes for games; prioritize ankle/foot strength, landing mechanics, and controlled unilateral work (since **2026-02-27**). Monitor symptoms and any “giving way.”
- **[ACTIVE]** In-season fatigue/soreness tolerance — training cannot create excessive soreness that impacts basketball performance (since **2026-02-27**).

### Preferences
- **Training emphasis:** Explosiveness + durability + injury prevention (lifting complements basketball; not maximal hypertrophy blocks)
- **Programming tolerance:** Moderate volume, high quality reps; avoid high-soreness eccentric overload and excessive novelty during season
- **Session style constraint:** Must fit **45 min** including warm-up

---

## METRICS
> **Data not provided yet** (as of **2026-02-27**). Add baseline testing/training numbers when available.

### Strength (basketball-supportive)
- Not yet recorded: key lifts (e.g., trap bar deadlift, front squat, bench, chin-ups), rep ranges, and dates

### Endurance (sport context)
- Not yet recorded: conditioning tests (if tracked), minutes load, or HR data

### Movement Quality (priority given ankle constraint)
- Not yet recorded: single-leg balance, ankle ROM (dorsiflexion), hop/landing tolerance, pain ratings

### Body Composition
- Not yet recorded: bodyweight (as of date), height, body fat % (optional)

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created / in-season context captured
- Set primary goal to **in-season performance support** with **12 weeks remaining**.
- Confirmed training availability: **Mon/Thu, 2:00 PM, 45 min, 2x/week**.
- Equipment confirmed: university performance center with **platforms, racks, barbells/dumbbells, sled, plyo boxes**.
- Added constraints: **chronic ankle instability** (taped for games) + **must minimize soreness/fatigue** to protect on-court performance.
- Metrics section pending: requested future capture of baseline lift numbers, bodyweight, and ankle/movement assessments.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** In-Season Strength + Power Maintenance (2-Day) — Basketball Performance & Ankle Durability  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** Brandon Taylor  
- **Goal:** In-season performance support (maintain/build strength & power with minimal soreness; improve explosiveness; durability/injury prevention; ankle stability)  
- **Duration:** 12 weeks (in-season window), then **ongoing with phase cycling**

---

## Program Philosophy
Brandon is an intermediate lifter and in-season D3 basketball athlete with limited lift availability (Mon/Thu, 45 min) and a high priority on staying fresh for practice/games. This program uses **two concise full-body sessions** that emphasize **power first** (low volume, high intent), then **strength maintenance** through a few key patterns (hinge, squat pattern, press, pull) with **moderate RPE** to avoid DOMS. Chronic ankle instability is addressed every session via **foot/ankle isometrics, controlled single-leg work, and landing mechanics**—enough to build robustness without creating soreness. Progression is **autoregulated (RPE + small weekly jumps)** with planned “down” weeks to match in-season fatigue and keep performance stable.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation + Tissue Tolerance (Weeks 1–4)
**Intent:** Establish repeatable in-season lifts, reinforce landing mechanics, build ankle/foot capacity, and find conservative training loads (minimal soreness).

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Power + Hinge Strength (Full Body A)**  
- **Focus:** Jump/throw power + posterior chain strength; controlled unilateral for ankle stability  
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets (+ low-volume plyos)

1) **Pogo Jumps (ankle stiffness) or Jump Rope:** 2 × 15–25 sec @ easy-moderate  
   *Builds ankle stiffness/reactivity with very low soreness cost.*

2) **Box Jump (stick landing):** 3 × 2–3 reps @ full rest, RPE 6–7 (quality only)  
   *Reinforces safe landing + power without high eccentric load.*

3) **Trap Bar Deadlift (preferred in-season):** 3 × 4–5 @ RPE 7  
   *High force output with generally lower soreness and easier technique than straight-bar pulls.*

4) **DB Bench Press (neutral grip if shoulders prefer):** 3 × 6 @ RPE 7

5) **Chest-Supported Row (DB/machine):** 3 × 8 @ RPE 7  
   *Upper-back strength for contact, posture, and shoulder health.*

6) **Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (controlled tempo):** 2 × 6 each @ RPE 6–7  
   *Unilateral control + hip strength; keep reps low to avoid DOMS.*

7) **Ankle Stability Superset (quick):**  
   - **Tibialis Raises (against wall or machine):** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
   - **Single-Leg Balance (eyes forward):** 2 × 20–30 sec each  
   *Targets common ankle/foot weak links for “giving way.”*

---

**Thu — Accel/Power + Squat Pattern Strength (Full Body B)**  
- **Focus:** Acceleration intent + squat pattern + pull strength; ankle/foot robustness  
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets (+ low-volume sled)

1) **Sled Push (accel emphasis):** 4–6 × 10–15 m @ heavy but fast, full walk-back rest  
   *Acceleration-specific power with minimal muscle damage.*

2) **Med Ball Scoop Toss or Chest Pass (if available):** 3 × 3–5 @ max intent  
   *Upper-body power without fatigue (optional if equipment exists).*

3) **Front Squat (or Goblet Squat if needed):** 3 × 3–5 @ RPE 7  
   *Squat strength maintenance with less absolute loading than back squat.*

4) **Pull-Ups / Chin-Ups (assisted if needed):** 3 × 5–8 @ RPE 7

5) **DB RDL:** 2 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
   *Hamstring strength for sprinting/jumping; moderate volume to avoid soreness.*

6) **Half-Kneeling Landmine Press (or DB OHP):** 2–3 × 6 @ RPE 7  
   *Shoulder-friendly pressing + trunk control.*

7) **Foot/Ankle Finisher (pick 1–2):**  
   - **Soleus Isometric (knee bent calf raise hold):** 2 × 20–30 sec each  
   - **Single-Leg Calf Raise (controlled):** 1–2 × 8–10 each @ RPE 7  
   *Soleus capacity is key for decel/landing and ankle resilience.*

#### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main lifts (trap bar DL, front squat, DB bench):** use a **double progression**:  
  - Start Week 1 at ~RPE 6–7.  
  - Add **+1 rep per set** week-to-week until top of range, then add **+5–10 lb** (lower) / **+2.5–5 lb** (upper).  
- **Power work:** progress by **quality** (crisper landings, higher intent), not by volume. Keep jumps at **≤ 9 total reps** per day.  
- **Week 4 deload (recommended):** keep intensity similar but reduce volume ~40–50% (e.g., 2 sets instead of 3; fewer accessories).  
- **Move to Phase 2** if: soreness stays low, bar speed remains good, and ankle feels stable (no “giving way” events during training).

---

# Phase 2: In-Season Build (Strength-Power Emphasis) (Weeks 5–8)
**Intent:** Slightly heavier strength exposure (still not grinding) + maintain/boost power outputs.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Power + Heavy Hinge (A2)**  
- **Focus:** CNS-ready power + heavier hinge; minimal accessory fatigue  
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

1) **Box Jump (stick):** 4 × 2 @ full rest, RPE 6–7  
2) **Trap Bar Deadlift:** 4 × 3 @ RPE 7–8 (leave ~2 reps in reserve)  
3) **DB Bench Press:** 3 × 5 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Chest-Supported Row:** 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Single-Leg Step-Down (slow):** 2 × 5–6 each @ RPE 6–7  
   *Decel control and knee-over-toe tolerance supporting ankle stability/landing.*  
6) **Tibialis Raise + Soleus Iso:** 2 rounds (short rests)

---

**Thu — Speed + Squat Strength (B2)**  
- **Focus:** Sled speed/accel + squat exposure; maintain pull strength  
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

1) **Sled Push:** 6 × 10 m @ fast, moderate-heavy (no grind)  
2) **Front Squat:** 4 × 3 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Chin-Ups/Pull-Ups:** 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **DB RDL or Hip Thrust:** 2 × 6 @ RPE 7  
   *Choose the one that creates less soreness for you mid-season.*  
5) **Landmine Press:** 2–3 × 5–6 @ RPE 7–8  
6) **Single-Leg Balance (add reach):** 2 × 20–30 sec each  
   *Progress balance by adding reach patterns before adding fatigue.*

#### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts:** aim for small load increases weekly (**+5–10 lb** lower, **+2.5–5 lb** upper) *only if* RPE stays within target and practice performance isn’t impacted.  
- **Cap intensity:** no missed reps; avoid RPE 9–10 in-season.  
- **Week 8 deload:** cut sets by ~40–50%, keep jumps/sled but reduce to 2–3 sets.

---

# Phase 3: Realization / Peak Freshness (Weeks 9–12)
**Intent:** Maintain strength and power with the lowest effective dose; keep legs “springy,” protect ankles, and prioritize game readiness.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Neural Prime (A3)**  
- **Focus:** Power + low-volume heavy exposure  
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets

1) **Pogo Jumps or Low Hurdle Hops:** 2 × 15–20 sec  
2) **Box Jump:** 3 × 2 @ very high quality  
3) **Trap Bar Deadlift:** 3 × 2 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **DB Bench:** 3 × 4–5 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Row (any stable variation):** 2–3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
6) **Ankle Complex (quick):** tibialis raises + soleus iso, 1–2 rounds

---

**Thu — Speed/Strength Maintenance (B3)**  
- **Focus:** Sled speed + squat pattern practice; minimal fatigue  
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets

1) **Sled Push:** 4–5 × 10 m @ fast, stop before slowdown  
2) **Front Squat:** 3 × 2–3 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Chins/Pull-Ups:** 3 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Landmine Press (or Push-Up weighted):** 2 × 5–6 @ RPE 7  
5) **Step-Down or Split Squat (very light):** 1–2 × 5 each @ RPE 6  
6) **Balance/Foot:** 1–2 short rounds

#### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Progress by **speed and crispness**, not volume.  
- Maintain loads; if you feel great, add **small** jumps (+2.5–5 lb upper, +5 lb lower).  
- **If games/practices intensify:** reduce lower-body accessory work first, then reduce one set from squats/deads as needed.  
- **Week 12 taper option (if playoffs/end-of-season push):** keep intensity, cut volume ~50% (e.g., 2×2 on main lifts, minimal accessories).

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Order:** Foundation (4 wks) → Build (4 wks) → Realization (4 wks) → repeat as needed during long seasons  
- **Transition triggers:**  
  - Time-based (end of each 4-week block) **or** readiness-based (if soreness/fatigue rises, transition early to the lower-volume phase).  
- **In future off-season:** shift to a higher-volume strength/hypertrophy block and more aggressive progression; in-season remains “minimum effective dose.”

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created for in-season basketball constraints (2 days/week, 45 min). Emphasis set to power-first + strength maintenance + ankle stability with planned deloads to minimize DOMS and protect practice/game performance.

--- 

If you tell me your **typical game days**, how hard Monday practice usually is, and any preferred main lifts (trap bar vs straight bar, front squat vs safety bar), I can fine-tune exercise order and the deload weeks to match your weekly competitive rhythm.</Plan>

**Duration:** ~45 minutes

1. Row or bike: **2:00** easy
2. Movement prep (1 round):
   - High knees: **20 yd**
   - Butt kicks: **20 yd**
   - Lateral shuffle: **10 yd each way**
3. Ankle/foot prep (1 round):
   - Single-leg pogos in place: **10/side** (low amplitude)
   - Heel-elevated bodyweight squat: **8 reps** (easy ROM)
4. Front rack prep:
   - Empty bar front squat hold in rack position: **2 × 10 sec**

**Main Workout**

### 1. Sled Push (acceleration emphasis)
**Target:** 5 × 12 m @ heavy but fast (no grind), full walk-back rest  
- **Set 1:** 180 lb total on sled × 12 m (fast)  
- **Set 2:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 3:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 4:** 180 lb × 12 m  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 12 m  
**Rest:** Walk-back + 45–60 sec (full recovery)  
**Notes:** Forward shin angle, punch the ground back, torso ~45°. If speed drops, reduce to **160**.

### 2. Front Squat
**Target:** 3 × 5 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 95 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 135 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 6:** 165 lb × 5 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2:30 between working sets  
**Notes:** Upright torso, elbows high. This is a “practice strength” set—no grinding.

### 3. Chin-Ups (or Pull-Ups)
**Target:** 3 × 6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** Bodyweight × 6 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full hang to chin over bar; if you miss reps, switch to **band-assisted** and finish the rep target.

### 4. DB Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70s × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 2:** 70s × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hinge back, lats tight, stop at mid-shin (no stretch-chasing).

### 5. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb (bar + 10) × 6/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 45 lb (bar + 10) × 6/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ribcage down, squeeze glute on kneeling side, press slightly up-and-forward.

### 6. Foot/Ankle Finisher
**Target:** 2 rounds  
- **A) Soleus Isometric (bent-knee calf raise hold):** 25 sec/side  
- **B) Single-Leg Calf Raise (controlled):** 8 reps/side @ bodyweight  
**Rest:** 30 sec between moves  
**Notes:** Soleus burn is normal; keep ankle stacked (don’t roll outward).

**Cool Down (4 minutes)**
1. Quad stretch: **30 sec/side**  
2. Adductor rock-backs: **8 reps/side**  
3. Lat/pec stretch (doorway or rig): **30 sec/side**  
4. Ankle dorsiflexion wall rocks: **10/side** (gentle)

**Notes**
- If you have a game within 24–36 hours, keep front squat at **2 working sets** and skip DB RDL.  
- Goal is leaving with legs feeling “charged,” not cooked.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Mon (Power + Hinge):** Established conservative baselines (Trap Bar **255×5×3**, DB Bench **70s×6×3**) with low-soreness single-leg work and ankle stability.
- **Thu (Accel + Squat):** Sled work stayed speed-focused (5×12 m @ **180 lb**), front squat baseline **165×5×3**, pulling volume kept moderate for recovery.
- **Constraint monitoring (ankle + in-season fatigue):** Daily ankle work included (tibialis + balance + soleus). Priority this week: **no wobble landings, no grinders**, and keep split squat/RDL controlled to limit DOMS.
- **Weak points (durability):** Consistent ankle stiffness (pogos) + soleus capacity (isos) + unilateral control (RFESS) baked in without adding volume.
- **Next week (Week 2):** If practices feel normal and soreness is minimal, add **+1 rep per set** on trap bar (to **3×6**) *or* add **+5 lb** while staying at 3×5; keep jumps/sled volume the same and progress by crispness.</Week>
